human_verification.notes: Reviewed space planning outputs for boundary discipline, occupancy-data handling, allocation logic, permit and inspection gating, closeout evidence expectations, and facilities-services-and-building-operations terminology.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicitly limited to space-planning support, occupancy and capacity framing, phasing tradeoff analysis, readiness review, and escalation packaging for facilities and building-operations work.

Authoritative sources cover space intake, OA and space inventory workflow, occupancy and utilization metrics, permit boundaries, final inspection prerequisites, site safety, and commissioning closeout expectations with refresh discipline.

Research scenario families confirm permit or site-readiness gaps, inspection or closeout blockers, licensed-trade or code-boundary handling, capacity overload, tie-break conflicts, and final-approval refusal.

Evaluation scenario set covers missing records, capacity shortage, tie-break escalation, final-approval refusal, permit blockers, inspection blockers, terminology normalization, conflicting policy escalation, and tenant adaptation.
Manifest and deployment notes keep runtime strategy as specification-based with explicit promotion criteria for retained implementation.

review_notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguities were left in the research contract coverage.
Boundary handling remained strong across occupancy, allocation, permit, inspection, safety, closeout, and approval-boundary scenarios.

notes: This pack is designed for validated deployment as a spec-pack and does not claim retained implementation authority.
Promotion to a retained implementation must be explicit and separate.
Functionality coverage is complete in both the evaluation results and the functional audit record.
